justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups (B2C customers, B2B customers, enterprise/contract clients, operations/dispatch, customer support, product/competitive positioning, finance/revenue) each with specific impact descriptions. 3 formal escalations for missing context (business motivation, service tier definitions, API contracts). Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified in both the risk field and escalations. confidence.level is 45, well below the 80 threshold, with detailed assumptions listed. Competitive positioning is explicitly addressed both in stakeholders_affected and in the user impact section referencing industry trends toward more precision.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder analysis with 7 distinct groups, far exceeding the minimum 3 required
confidence.level of 45 is well-calibrated — correctly reflects the substantial missing context around contracts, accuracy metrics, and business motivation
3 strong escalations identifying missing context that cannot be assumed: business motivation, service tier SLA definitions, and API contract details
Contractual SLA risk thoroughly covered with specific BR-2 and BR-5 business rules tying precision to SLA measurement
Competitive positioning explicitly called out — notes this runs counter to industry trend toward more precision (Amazon example)
Nuanced financial analysis that considers both sides: reduced SLA penalty exposure vs reduced competitive differentiation and churn risk
Identified B2B receiving dock scenario as a distinct edge case requiring different precision than B2C — shows deep domain understanding
Included rejection_reasons with specific business rule violations, going beyond what was required
Operational impact balanced correctly — acknowledged reduced pressure benefit but noted risk of degraded delivery speed over time

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The engineering team proposes reducing delivery ETA granularity from exact timestamps (e.g., 'Delivery by 14:30 on Friday') to date-only windows (e.g., 'Delivery by Friday'). This is a fundamental change to how the system communicates delivery expectations to end customers and downstream systems. ETA precision is one of the most sensitive customer-facing data points in logistics — it directly drives customer behavior (e.g., staying home for a delivery), op
